sourceMovie.title: Eagle Eye
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Eagle Eye for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. D.J. Caruso keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The mystery and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Salt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eagle Eye clicked for you, Salt (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Die Hard: With a Vengeance
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eagle Eye clicked for you, Die Hard: With a Vengeance (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eagle Eye?
faq.answer: Try Salt, Die Hard: With a Vengeance, Die Hard 2—each shares DNA with Eagle Eye while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eagle Eye?
faq.answer: Eagle Eye (2008) is best described as Mystery, Thriller, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eagle Eye worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this mystery lane.
